body.title: Experiment findings log — queryable record of what we tried and learned
body.result: infra.experiment_findings table + findings.py CLI (add/list/search/show). Seeded DeepSeek vs Qwen result. 8 tests.
body.description:
We ran a DeepSeek vs Qwen reading comparison. Qwen won by one unit extracted. That finding exists only in a compacted CC conversation — neither Igor nor CC can find it.

## Problem
- experiment_queue table is for Igor's runtime micro-experiments (probe/observe cycle)
- decisions_log.dsb is a flat file (and CC is told never to write to it)
- Session records capture what was done, not what was learned
- No queryable store for 'we tried X, Y won, here's the evidence'

## Solution
A Postgres table (infra schema) for experiment findings:
- experiment_id, title, hypothesis, method, result, conclusion
- participants (igor, cc, akien)
- evidence_refs (memory IDs, commit hashes, session IDs)
- created_at, created_by
- tags for searchability

Both CC and Igor deposit findings. Both can query. Comms channel comms://log/experiments gets a line per finding for the file-based trail.

## Not the same as
- experiment_queue (runtime micro-experiments with probe/observe)
- decisions_log (architectural decisions, not experimental findings)
- session records (what happened, not what we learned)

## Depends on
- T-uc-schema-three-namespaces (done — infra schema exists)
